news.headline: HCL Tech inks $1.14 billion AI-led digital transformation deal with Fortune Global 50 firm
news.what_happened: HCL Technologies announced a landmark partnership with a Europe-headquartered Fortune Global 50 company to build an AI-driven operating model managing their Global Digital Workplace and Enterprise Networks. The deal, estimated at $1.14 billion over the initial term, represents entirely new business for HCL Tech.
news.why_it_matters: Direct multi-year revenue catalyst adding significant visibility to HCL Tech's order book. Demonstrates continued enterprise demand for large-scale AI-driven managed services and re-rates the stock after a recent 52-week low, validating the AI-services narrative and boosting long-term EPS outlook.

news.headline: Gold surges +1.63% to $4,193/oz; Silver jumps +3.44% to $63.17 — precious metals in broad rally
news.what_happened: Precious metals surged sharply overnight following weaker-than-expected US June Nonfarm Payrolls (only 57,000 jobs added versus 150,000+ expected). Markets interpreted this as a cooling labor market, triggering a broad safe-haven rally in gold and silver as Fed rate hike bets were rapidly pared back.
news.why_it_matters: Gold at $4,193/oz directly boosts collateral values for gold-backed loan NBFCs. Higher gold prices mean larger loan disbursements, improved LTV headroom, lower NPA risk, and accelerated AUM growth for Muthoot Finance and Manappuram Finance. The rally also signals global risk-off, supporting defensive positioning.

news.headline: US June Nonfarm Payrolls: 57,000 jobs added — major miss vs expectations; September Fed rate hike bets pared
news.what_happened: The US Bureau of Labor Statistics reported only 57,000 nonfarm jobs added in June 2026, far below consensus estimates of 150,000+. Investors rapidly reduced bets on a September Federal Reserve rate hike following the data release.
news.why_it_matters: A less-hawkish Fed maintains supportive global liquidity conditions, reducing pressure on emerging market capital outflows. This is a broad positive for Indian markets — supportive for banks, NBFCs, IT stocks, and real estate. Also explains the gold/silver surge as dollar softens.

news.headline: Asian markets broadly positive: Nikkei +1.47%, Hang Seng +1.28%, China CSI 300 +1.15%, Kospi +4.65%
news.what_happened: Asian equity markets rallied broadly on Friday, led by Japan Nikkei +1.47%, South Korea Kospi surging 4.65%, Hong Kong Hang Seng +1.28%, and China CSI 300 +1.15%. Australia ASX 200 also gained +1.39%. Drivers: lower crude, supportive US jobs data narrative, and improving global risk appetite.
news.why_it_matters: Strong Asia risk-on sentiment provides a positive cue for Indian markets. Hang Seng strength reflects improving China industrial outlook — China exports surged +19.6% YoY in May (semiconductors +110%) — a direct demand signal for Indian steel and aluminum stocks.

news.headline: India-US interim trade deal enters final stage; tariff to fall to 18%; pharma gets zero duty
news.what_happened: Following PM Modi's G7 meeting with President Trump, India-US trade negotiations entered the final stage. The interim deal would apply an 18% reciprocal tariff on most Indian goods and remove tariffs entirely on generic pharmaceuticals and aircraft parts. Commerce Minister Goyal set to meet USTR Greer next week with July 24 target.
news.why_it_matters: A completed India-US trade deal is a major structural positive for Indian export sectors including pharma, IT, textiles, and engineering goods. Markets will continue pricing in a favorable outcome, creating sustained sentiment support for these sectors.

news.headline: India GST Collections June 2026 rise +13.9% YoY reflecting strong domestic economic activity
news.what_happened: India's GST collections for June 2026 grew 13.9% year-on-year, driven by higher import revenues, improved tax compliance, and sustained domestic economic activity across manufacturing and services.
news.why_it_matters: Confirms domestic consumption resilience and healthy fiscal trajectory. Reduces risk of government spending cuts and supports the broader market narrative of India's sustained economic growth. Positive backdrop for FMCG, consumer discretionary, and financial sector stocks.

sector_outlook.reason: HCL Tech $1.14B AI deal; weak US jobs = less hawkish Fed; India-US trade deal nearing
Gold +1.63% to $4,193/oz — directly boosts AUM, LTV headroom, loan disbursements
Hang Seng +1.28%, Nikkei +1.47%, China exports +19.6% YoY — Asia risk-on supports steel/aluminum
Supportive global liquidity; DII strong buying ₹1,784 Cr; weak US jobs = no near-term rate headwind
India-US trade deal near completion; zero duty on generics; USD/INR flat (no currency headwind)
Crude flat/+0.50% — no material jet fuel cost catalyst either way
Crude oil flat — no significant input cost relief or pressure
Strong GST data positive for demand; no significant input cost driver today
No India-specific catalyst; global liquidity support is indirect
Natural gas +1.75% to $3.252 — increases feedstock costs for urea/DAP manufacturers

picks.catalyst: HCL Tech inks $1.14 billion AI-led digital transformation deal with Fortune Global 50 firm
picks.logic: Entirely new business win of $1.14B over initial term — direct multi-year revenue catalyst with strong order book visibility. Stock recently hit 52-week lows; this deal is a fundamental re-rating trigger. Weak US nonfarm payrolls (57K) keeps Fed less hawkish, sustaining US enterprise tech spending environment that benefits HCL Tech's managed services vertical.

picks.catalyst: Gold surges +1.63% to $4,193/oz on weak US jobs data and Fed rate hike pause bets
picks.logic: India's largest gold loan NBFC. Gold price rise has direct P&L impact: higher collateral values enable larger loan disbursements, improve LTV buffer safety (lower NPA risk), and accelerate AUM growth. Gold at near-record $4,193/oz provides strong fundamental support for loan book expansion. Stock has corrected in recent weeks, offering attractive entry.

picks.catalyst: Gold +1.63% to $4,193/oz — higher-beta gold loan NBFC play on precious metals rally
picks.logic: Second-largest gold loan NBFC with higher price sensitivity to gold moves than Muthoot Finance. Gold rally boosts collateral LTV headroom, enabling higher disbursements and improved AUM quality. Manappuram offers leveraged exposure to the gold price rally within the NBFC space, suitable for investors seeking higher beta to the gold catalyst.

picks.catalyst: Asia risk-on: Hang Seng +1.28%, Nikkei +1.47%; China exports +19.6% YoY signals strong industrial demand
picks.logic: Tata Steel's global operations benefit from positive steel demand signals from Asia. China's surging exports (semiconductors +110%, overall exports +19.6% YoY in May) signal robust industrial activity — a leading indicator for global steel pricing. Hang Seng rally reflects improving China sentiment. Broad Asia risk-on improves near-term steel price outlook.

risks: US markets closed for July 4 Independence Day holiday — thin global liquidity, lower volumes expected today
GIFT Nifty already +0.74% — much of positive news may be priced in at open; avoid chasing gap-ups
FII mild net selling (-₹311.80 Cr) could persist — monitor intraday FII flow updates
Friday effect: end-of-week profit booking and position squaring may cap afternoon gains
Natural gas +1.75% — headwind for fertilizer stocks (COROMANDEL, GNFC, DEEPAKFERT — avoid today)
Gold +1.63% signals global risk-off undercurrent — if sustained, broader market may rotate to defensives
